summary: Documentation-only PR adding a GitHub pull request template that points contributors to CONTRIBUTING.md, and replacing the prior Discord notification sentence in CONTRIBUTING.md with an AI Assistance section. The new guidance says AI use for matching functions is acceptable, but discourages AI-driven naming of struct fields or globally accessible identifiers, discourages AI attempts to match data sections, and asks contributors not to include AI output in PR/issue bodies/comments or automated code reviews except for relevant concise AI summaries marked as quoted markdown.

key_files.role: Contributor guidelines updated with an AI Assistance section and removal of the prior Discord notification sentence.
New GitHub PR template directing submitters to read the contributing guidelines before submitting.

what_changed.text: Added an AI Assistance section to CONTRIBUTING.md stating that using AI to match functions is acceptable and frequently practiced.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:.github/CONTRIBUTING.md: 'Using AI to match functions is acceptable and frequently practiced.'

what_changed.text: Added guidance to refrain from allowing AI to name struct fields or other globally accessible identifiers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:.github/CONTRIBUTING.md: 'Refrain from allowing AI to name fields in structs or other globally accessible identifiers.'

what_changed.text: Added guidance to discourage AI from attempting to match data sections and to focus solely on code matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:.github/CONTRIBUTING.md: 'Discourage AI from attempting to match data sections, instead focusing solely on code matches.'

what_changed.text: Added guidance to avoid including AI output in PR/issue bodies/comments and to avoid automated code reviews, with a narrow allowance for relevant concise AI summaries wrapped as markdown quotes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:.github/CONTRIBUTING.md: AI output and automated code review bullet

what_changed.text: Created .github/pull_request_template.md with a one-line reminder to read CONTRIBUTING.md before submitting a pull request.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:.github/pull_request_template.md: 'Before submitting a pull request, please read our [contributing guidelines](./CONTRIBUTING.md).'

smart_moves.text: Put AI-use policy in CONTRIBUTING.md and linked it from the PR template, making the guidance visible before PR submission.
Kept the PR template minimal, reducing review burden while routing contributors to the fuller guideline document.

decomp_lessons.text: Project documentation in this PR explicitly permits AI assistance for matching functions, but frames AI as unsuitable for naming struct fields or globally accessible identifiers.
Project documentation in this PR explicitly prefers AI focus on code matches rather than data-section matching.

naming_conventions.text: AI should not be allowed to name fields in structs or other globally accessible identifiers.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: When using AI assistance, direct it toward function/code matching rather than data-section matching.

follow_up_queries: Refrain from allowing AI to name fields in structs
Discourage AI from attempting to match data sections
avoid including AI output in pull request body comments

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI assistance is acceptable for code matching but not for naming global-facing identifiers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2513 added contributor documentation stating that AI use for matching functions is acceptable, while contributors should refrain from allowing AI to name struct fields or other globally accessible identifiers.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI-assisted work should avoid data-section matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2513 added contributor guidance to discourage AI from attempting to match data sections and to focus solely on code matches.

source_update_candidates.title: Document project-level AI assistance boundaries
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-referencing a standard that captures the PR 2513 contributor guidance: AI assistance may be used for matching functions, but should not be relied on to name struct fields or globally accessible identifiers, should be discouraged from data-section matching, and should not submit automated reviews or unmarked AI output in PR/issue discussion.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR added repository contributor guidance that overlaps with current decomp standards on conservative naming and text-before-data matching; curator may want an explicit source-owned AI-assistance standard or cross-reference.

source_update_candidates.title: GitHub PR template points to CONTRIBUTING.md
source_update_candidates.text: .github/pull_request_template.md is a GitHub PR template containing a reminder to read .github/CONTRIBUTING.md before submitting a pull request.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a repository workflow/path fact introduced by the PR and may be useful for future PR-process searches.
